remember: Students will be able to recall the geographical location and setting of Vijayanagara.
Students will be able to identify the key features of the city and its surroundings.
Students will be able to list the various monuments and structures built during the Vijayanagara period.
understand: Students will be able to explain the reasons behind the choice of location for Vijayanagara.
Students will be able to describe the layout and planning of the city.
Students will be able to discuss the significance of the city's layout and planning in the context of its role as an imperial capital.
apply: Students will be able to use historical evidence to explain the role of Vijayanagara as an imperial capital.
Students will be able to apply their knowledge of the city's geography and layout to understand its role in the regional and national context.
Students will be able to use historical examples to demonstrate the impact of Vijayanagara as an imperial capital on the surrounding regions.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the various factors that contributed to the rise and decline of Vijayanagara as an imperial capital.
Students will be able to evaluate the impact of Vijayanagara on the regional and national politics of the time period.
Students will be able to compare and contrast Vijayanagara with other imperial capitals of the time period.

ncert_objectives: To understand the geographical setting of Vijayanagara and its significance in the regional context.
To analyze the layout and planning of the city and its role in the imperial capital.
To discuss the various monuments and structures built during the Vijayanagara period and their significance in the context of the city's role as an imperial capital.
board_exam_objectives: To describe the geographical setting of Vijayanagara and its significance in the regional context.
To explain the role of Vijayanagara as an imperial capital and its impact on the surrounding regions.
To analyze the various factors that contributed to the rise and decline of Vijayanagara as an imperial capital.
